revision.text:
[[ja:大将軍八神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Daishogun Hachi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Daishogunhachi-jinja Kyoto Japan04s3.jpg | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[Kamigyō Ward]], [[Ichijō Street]], West of [[Gozen Street]], Nishimachi 3-55 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.026666666666664|N|135.73394444444443|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Daishōgun|qid=Q167008|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)}} | Shrine Rank = Former [[:en:Fusha|Fusha]] | Foundation = {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|qid=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 13 (794 CE) | Main hall style = [[Gongen-zukuri]] | Affiliation = [[National Seisokukai]] (a pilgrimage of 59 shrines dedicated to Susanoo-no-Mikoto) | Annual festival = | Sacred rites = [[Tenmon Festival]] (Third Sunday of October) | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Daishogun
Hachi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|1|36.0|N|135|44|2.2|E|qq=}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|qid=Q1321824|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|qid=Q3155268|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} }} '''Daishogun Hachi Shrine''' (大将軍八神社, Daishōgun Hachi Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Kamigyō Ward]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]]. It primarily enshrines [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] as its main deity. Originally, the shrine's principal deity was the {{ill|Daishōgun (directional deity)|ja|大将軍 (方位神)|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishogun (directional deity)|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishogun (directional deity)|qq=}}. The "Hachi Shrine" in the name refers to the eight deities of [[Onmyōdō]]'s {{ill|Hasshōjin|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshōjin|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, later associated with the eight offspring deities of Susanoo-no-Mikoto. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|qid=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 13 (794 CE) during the relocation of the capital to {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|qid=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō|1=Heian-kyō}}. Based on {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|qid=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}}, the shrine was constructed to protect the capital's [[Tengate]] (northwest gate), a vulnerable direction. The directional deity {{ill|Daishōgun (directional deity)|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishogun (directional deity)|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishogun (directional deity)|qq=}} was enshrined, who personifies {{ill|Venus|en|Venus|qid=Q313|lt=Venus|1=Venus}} (Taibai) and serves as a directional guardian deity. The earliest documented reference to the shrine is in the {{ill|Sankaiki|ja|山槐記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankaiki|1=Sankaiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11469947}}, which mentions it among 41 shrines receiving offerings for the safe childbirth of [[Empress Consort Kenrei-mon-in]] in {{ill|1178|en|1178|qid=Q19696|lt=1178|1=1178}} (Jishō 2). During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, the shrine fell into disrepair due to the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|qid=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}} but was later restored. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, as worship of the {{ill|Hasshōjin|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshōjin|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}} grew, Daishogun was syncretized with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (also associated with [[Gozu Tennō]]). The Hasshōjin were also reinterpreted as the offspring deities of Susanoo, or the {{ill|Hachioji_Gongen|ja|八王子権現|lt=Eight Kings|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji_Gongen|1=Hachiōji Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11391615}} (八王子) of Gozu Tennō. Consequently, the shrine's name evolved to Daishogun Hachimiyagū, later simplified to Daishogun Hachi Shrine. During the Meiji period, under the [[:en:Shinbutsu Bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the official deities of the shrine were recorded as Susanoo-no-Mikoto and his eight offspring deities. The shrine was designated as a [[:en:Fusha|Fusha]]. The shrine is popular for its association with directional protection, attracting devotees seeking blessings for construction, relocation, and travel. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Daishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishogun (directional deity)|qq=}} (as a directional deity, identified with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|qid=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}); as one of the eight child deities, identified with {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|qid=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone|1=Amatsuhikone}}) * {{ill|Eight Directional Deities|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}} (Eight Child Deities) ** {{ill|Taisai-shin|ja|太歳神|1=Taisaijin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisai-shin|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisaijin|qq=}} ({{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|qid=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}) ** {{ill|Taion-shin|ja|太陰神|1=Taionjin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taion-shin|13=User:Immanuelle/Taionjin|qq=}} ({{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵嶋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}) ** {{ill|Saigyōshin|ja|歳刑神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigyōshin|qq=}} ({{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田心媛命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}) ** {{ill|Saiha Shin|ja|歳破神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saiha Shin|qq=}} ({{ill|Tatsutahime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsutahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}) ** {{ill|Saisetsujin|ja|歳殺神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisetsujin|qq=}} ({{ill|Ame-no-Hohiwake|ja|天穂日神|1=Ame no Hohi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohiwake|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}) ** {{ill|Ōbanjin|ja|黄幡神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbanjin|qq=}} ({{ill|Katsuhiko-ne|ja|活津彦根神|1=Ikutsuhikone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuhiko-ne|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イクツヒコネ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イクツヒコネ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11147583}}) ** {{ill|Hyōbijin|ja|豹尾神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbijin|qq=}} ({{ill|Kumano Kusubi no Mikoto|ja|熊野櫲樟日命|1=Kumanokusubi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanokusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クマノクスビ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クマノクスビ}}) * {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|qid=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}} * {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|qid=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}} == Grounds == * Main hall * Worship hall * [[Jishu Shrine]] - Enshrines Ōsugi Daimyōjin and Mamekichi Myōjin * Five Shrines - Includes Ebisu Shrine, Inari Shrine, Tenmangu Shrine, Chōja Shrine, and Konpira Shrine * Three Shrines - Includes Myōbu Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine, and Sarutahiko Shrine * Hōtoku Hall - Treasure hall housing 80 Important Cultural Property wooden statues of Daishogun. * Ōkanjin Shrine * Saitoku Shrine * Shrine office * Shrine gate File:Daishogunhachi-jinja Kyoto Japan01n.jpg|Torii and shrine gate File:Daishogunhachi-jinja Kyoto Japan10n.jpg|Three shrines File:Daishogunhachi Shrine.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Statue of Daishogun.jpg|Half-seated Daishogun statue in front of Hōtoku Hall File:大将軍八神社神輿.jpg|Portable shrine at the Tenmon Festival == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * 80 Wooden statues of Daishogun These statues, dating from the mid to late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, depict male gods in various forms, including armored figures, formal court attire, and one in child-like form. Despite their damages, this collection is unique in Japan. They were designated as Important Cultural Property in {{ill|1972|en|1972|qid=Q2476|lt=1972|1=1972}} and later expanded in {{ill|2003|en|2003|qid=Q1986|lt=2003|1=2003}} to include an additional statue found on-site. === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property === * Ancient astronomical and calendrical documents. The treasure hall "Hōtoku Hall" houses these items and is open to the public annually from May 1–5 and November 1–5 (reservation required on other dates). == Connection to Local names == The "Daishogun" district in Kyoto's {{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|en|Kita-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1154970|lt=Kita Ward, Kyoto|1=Kita-ku, Kyoto}} derives its name from this shrine. However, the shrine itself is located east of the {{ill|Tenjin River|en|Tenjin River|qid=Q16141827|lt=Tenjingawa River (Kyoto)|1=Tenjin River}}, outside the Daishogun district, and the readings of "Daishogun" also differ slightly. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == See also == {{Commonscat|Daishogunhachi-jinja|Daishogun Hachijinja|qq=}} * {{ill|Daishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishogun (directional deity)|qq=}} * {{ill|Eight Directional Deities|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}} * {{ill|Direction Gods|ja|方位神|1=Directional deity|1=Directional deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Direction Gods|13=User:Immanuelle/Directional deity|qq=}} * {{ill|Katamigae|ja|方違え|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katamigae|1=Katatagae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11504378}} * {{ill|Eho|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eho|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} * {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|qid=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}} * {{ill|Katamigae Shrine|ja|方違神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katamigae Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Okazaki Shrine|ja|岡崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachidaijin Shrine|ja|八大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachidaijin Shrine|1=Hachidai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17218563}} * {{ill|Yagami Shrine|ja|八神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Shrine|1=Hachi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q106852479}}, {{ill|Hachioji Shrine|ja|八王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八王子神社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八王子神社}} * {{ill|Daishōgun Shrine|ja|大将軍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishogun Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.daishogun.or.jp/ Daishogun Hachi Shrine (Official Website)] {{Shinto side|qq=}} {{Normdaten|qq=}} {{shinto-stub|qq=}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.daishogun.or.jp/ 大将軍八神社（公式サイト）] {{Normdaten|qq=}} {{shinto-stub|qq=}} {{pref-stub-京都府|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|大将軍八神社|version=102080716|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大将軍八神社]] {{Commons category|Daishogunhachi-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11434526}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]]
